quick_facts.label: Typical teaching stage
quick_facts.value: This role is typically the primary stage, Classes I–V; Sikkim’s official STET bulletin labels its primary-teacher eligibility category as “Classes I–V.”

quick_facts.label: Qualification framework
quick_facts.value: Under section 23 of the Right to Education Act, appointment eligibility depends on minimum qualifications laid down by the Central Government’s authorised academic authority; states then apply their own recruitment rules and notifications.

quick_facts.label: Common entry credentials
quick_facts.value: State TET plus an approved elementary-teacher-education qualification are common requirements. For example, Sikkim lists Senior Secondary plus D.El.Ed., B.El.Ed., or Diploma in Education (Special Education) routes for Classes I–V, while a 2026 Goa recruitment notice requires a recognised TET and specifies qualifying teacher-education routes.

quick_facts.label: Core statutory duties
quick_facts.value: Teachers must attend regularly and punctually, complete the curriculum, assess each child’s learning and provide additional instruction where needed, and meet parents or guardians regularly to communicate attendance and progress.

quick_facts.label: Pay and service conditions
quick_facts.value: Pay, allowances, and service conditions are prescribed rather than nationally uniform. As a dated example, Goa Staff Selection Commission’s 2026 Government Primary School Teacher advertisement listed Pay Level 6; this should not be treated as the scale in other states or future recruitments.

sections.heading: What the role involves
paragraphs: A state-government primary teacher delivers the prescribed elementary curriculum and is accountable for completing it within the required time. The statutory duty is not limited to classroom delivery: the teacher must assess each child’s learning ability and provide supplementary instruction where needed.

The role also includes regular attendance and punctuality, regular communication with parents or guardians about attendance and learning progress, and any additional duties prescribed under applicable service rules. The Right to Education Act also prohibits private tuition or private teaching activity by a teacher covered by the Act.
bullets: Plan and teach age-appropriate primary-level lessons.
Monitor individual learning and arrange additional support when required.
Maintain regular school attendance and complete the prescribed curriculum.
Meet parents or guardians regularly to discuss attendance, progress, and learning needs.
Work within child-centred curriculum and evaluation principles, including activity, discovery, and exploration-based learning.

sections.heading: Entry route: teacher education, State TET, and state recruitment
paragraphs: There is no single all-India appointment advertisement for this state-government role. The practical route is to satisfy the applicable minimum-qualification framework, pass the State TET accepted by the recruiting state, and then qualify through that state’s separate recruitment process. Candidates should treat the current state recruitment notification—not a generic eligibility summary—as controlling for age, reservation, language, documents, experience, selection stages, and posting conditions.

For the primary stage, official state materials illustrate common teacher-education pathways. Sikkim’s STET bulletin lists Senior Secondary plus a two-year D.El.Ed., a four-year B.El.Ed., or a two-year Diploma in Education (Special Education) for Classes I–V, and states that the relevant teacher-education diploma or degree must be recognised by NCTE (with special-education recognition noted separately). A 2026 Goa government primary-teacher recruitment notice similarly required a TET recognised by Goa and listed several qualification routes, but also required two years’ teaching experience and knowledge of Konkani. These differences show why candidates must read the notification for their target state and post.
bullets: Complete an accepted elementary-teacher-education qualification for the relevant state and vacancy.
Pass the relevant State TET for the primary stage; a TET pass establishes eligibility but does not itself create an appointment.
Apply only when a state department, school education directorate, staff selection commission, or other authorised recruiting body publishes a vacancy.
Check notification-specific requirements such as local-language ability, age limits and relaxations, category certificates, experience, document verification, and selection rules.
Verify that the qualification and institution recognition meet the notification’s requirements before applying.

sections.heading: Skills and knowledge to develop
paragraphs: The statutory responsibilities and State TET content point to a primary teacher’s core professional capabilities: child development and pedagogy, teaching through relevant languages, foundational mathematics and environmental studies, individual learning assessment, and communication with families. The particular classroom language and subject expectations can be state- and post-specific.

The Right to Education Act frames elementary curriculum and evaluation around the child’s all-round development, activity, discovery and exploration, a child-friendly and child-centred approach, and a learning environment free from fear, trauma, and anxiety. These principles make inclusive classroom practice, observation, formative assessment, and clear parent communication central to the role.
bullets: Child development, educational psychology, and primary pedagogy.
Teaching and communication in the appointed school’s required medium or languages.
Foundational literacy, numeracy, environmental-studies, and lesson-planning capability.
Observation, assessment, record-informed additional support, and differentiation for learners’ needs.
Constructive communication with parents or guardians and professional reliability.
Child-safe, child-centred, activity-based classroom practice.

sections.heading: Workplace and professional context
paragraphs: The workplace is a government elementary-school setting governed by the state’s education administration and applicable service rules. The RTE framework requires schools to meet prescribed norms and standards and requires the appropriate government or local authority to maintain the pupil–teacher ratio set out in the Act’s Schedule.

Teachers work with pupils, other school staff, and parents or guardians. School Management Committees for covered schools include teachers and are intended to monitor the school and prepare or recommend school-development plans; this places the role within a wider school-community structure rather than only an individual classroom.

The RTE Act generally protects teachers from non-educational deployment, except for the decennial census, disaster relief, and election-related duties. Actual deployment, transfer, attendance, language, and administrative practices remain subject to state rules and orders.
bullets: Primary classrooms serving elementary-age children, usually Classes I–V.
Regular collaboration with families and school-community structures.
Work shaped by state curriculum, service rules, recruitment conditions, and local language needs.
A statutory emphasis on child-centred learning and on maintaining prescribed school norms.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Do not use a single salary figure for this occupation nationally. Section 23 of the RTE Act leaves teacher salary, allowances, and service conditions to what is prescribed, so these vary by state, appointing authority, pay revision, appointment type, and the vacancy’s terms.

As a dated illustration only, Goa Staff Selection Commission’s Advertisement No. 01 of 2026 for Government Primary School Teacher (Konkani Medium) listed “Level - 6” and applications from January 30, 2026 through February 20, 2026. It is evidence of one recruitment’s stated pay level, not a benchmark for every state-government primary teacher role.
bullets: Check the exact recruitment advertisement for pay level or pay matrix, allowances, probation, pension or retirement coverage, and other service conditions.
Distinguish a regular sanctioned post from contractual, temporary, guest, or other engagement categories, because their compensation and benefits may differ.
Use the recruiting state’s current notification and service rules for any financial decision.
